questions.topic: Motion in One and Two Dimensions
standardRefs: NYS Physics
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Physics - Motion in One and Two Dimensions: which concept is the best anchor for a Regents-style item?
questions.correctText: displacement
choices: displacement
velocity
net force
tension
questions.explanation: displacement is the anchor concept for Motion in One and Two Dimensions. In NYS Physics, students need to use that concept to reason through a task, not just recognize the word.

questions.topic: Motion in One and Two Dimensions
standardRefs: NYS Physics
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student reviewing Physics: Motion in One and Two Dimensions needs to show the skill "resolve vectors." Which action best fits that skill?
questions.correctText: Use evidence to explain velocity.
choices: Use evidence to explain velocity.
Guess from key words about acceleration.
ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Repeat the definition of displacement only.
questions.explanation: The useful move is to connect evidence to velocity; this matches the standards/CED emphasis on reasoning and application.

questions.topic: Motion in One and Two Dimensions
standardRefs: NYS Physics
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which answer best avoids a common mistake about Physics: Motion in One and Two Dimensions?
questions.correctText: Connect displacement to the prompt context before choosing.
choices: Choose an unrelated fact from another unit.
using a rule from a different unit
Connect displacement to the prompt context before choosing.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: Most missed review questions come from using familiar terms without matching them to the exact context.

questions.topic: Motion in One and Two Dimensions
standardRefs: NYS Physics
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: In a Regents-style item about Motion in One and Two Dimensions, what kind of evidence would be strongest?

choices: ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Specific evidence that proves a claim about acceleration.
A fact from a different course.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: The standards across NYS courses and AP CEDs reward evidence used for a claim, not disconnected recall.
